feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: ACLU to monitor election certification as part of $50 million midterm effort
entry.content: Half of the group’s spending will go toward ensuring the proper administration of elections in key states amid Trump’s efforts to exert greater influence over the process.

entry.title: Trouble getting weight loss drugs covered by insurance? Here's what to know
entry.content: If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.

entry.title: Zohran Mamdani’s clout, a double Trump endorsement and more on the line in Tuesday’s primaries
entry.content: The parties will also set up a handful of key House battleground races as voters in Maryland, New York, South Carolina and Utah pick nominees.

entry.title: Is Harry Kane playing for England today? Projected lineup vs Ghana
entry.content: With Harry Kane on the brink of breaking an English World Cup record, will he play against Ghana? A look at the Three Lions as they ship up to Boston.

entry.title: Today’s Papers: Malago election reaction as Conte & Maldini linked with national team
entry.content: Here is a look at the key football stories making the front pages of Italy’s three major sports newspapers on Tuesday, June 23.Today’s PapersLa Gazzetta dello Sport leads with the FIGC presidentia...

entry.title: Madrid World Cup Spotlight: Kylian Mbappe looks unstoppable as France turn on the style against Iraq
entry.content: Real Madrid stars continue to set the stage alight at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Kylian Mbappe coming up with back-to-back braces in the tournament.France faced Iraq in an intriguing game that they...

entry.title: Barca World Cup Spotlight: Lionel Messi continues to defy age with another incredible display
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is the gift that keeps on giving, as it treated fans around the world to yet another spectacular set of games last night.Within the tournament itself, three players seem to be ...

entry.title: Daft Punk’s ‘One More Time’ becomes French team’s official 2026 World Cup goal song
entry.content: Les Bleus’ next World Cup game is set for tonight against Iraq, and their chosen soundtrack is already delighting music fans...View on euronews

entry.title: UFC unveils new 'Meta Rankings'
entry.content: UFC CEO Dana White has been critical of the UFC rankings system for years. Moving away from the traditional rankings compiled from media members, the fight promotion announced on Monday an AI generated ranking system powered by Meta. "As of June 22, 2026, the UFC will begin the transition of its ...

entry.title: World Cup 2026: Who are the ITV and BBC commentators, pundits and presenters?
entry.content: The star-studded line-ups aim to bring enthusiasm, insight and analysis across a packed summer of football

entry.title: Colombia vs. DR Congo projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group K game in Guadalajara
entry.content: Colombia and DR Congo will continue their push for a place in the World Cup knockout stages when they meet in their second Group K match.

entry.title: Darryn Peterson red flags, explained: How missed time, character issues at Kansas may impact NBA stock
entry.content: Here's a breakdown of the questions facing Peterson ahead of the draft.

entry.title: NBA Draft sleepers: 5 potential steals in 2026 including Tarris Reed, Meleek Thomas and Joshua Jefferson
entry.content: These players could be terrific values in the 2026 NBA Draft.

entry.title: Where to watch Special Olympics USA Games: TV schedule, times, channels, live streams for 2026 events
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the Special Olympics USA Games, including TV channel and streaming options for the 2026 event.

entry.title: Oil Prices Choppy Amid Mixed Signals From U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
entry.content: Oil prices were up in Asia, amid conflicting signals from talks between the U.S. and Iran on ending the conflict.

entry.title: Iran to let nuclear inspections resume as talks continue, Vance says
entry.content: Vance says Iran agreed to let international nuclear inspectors back into the country during a "very, very good" first day of negotiations with the U.S.

entry.title: England vs. Ghana projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup Group L game at Gillette Stadium
entry.content: England and Ghana will look to build upon their opening World Cup wins when they meet at Gillette Stadium on Tuesday.

entry.title: Where to watch Aces vs. Liberty today: Channel, time, TV schedule, live stream for Tuesday WNBA game
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about Aces vs. Liberty today, including TV channel and streaming options for the WNBA matchup.

entry.title: Celtics' massive Jaylen Brown-Giannis Antetokounmpo trade whiff is good news for Spurs
entry.content: The San Antonio Spurs received positive trade news on Monday night.

entry.title: Tyler Herro breaks silence on joining Bucks after Heat's Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
entry.content: The Bucks landed a player from Wisconsin in the Giannis Antetokounmpo deal.

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: In About-Face, Trump Administration Drops Opposition to Africa’s Antigay Laws
entry.content: The U.S. has been silent on a law passed in Ghana last month in a sharp shift from the Biden years.

entry.title: China’s Latest Tool to Control a Disputed Atoll: a Strange Floating Platform
entry.content: The movable structure has renewed fears that Beijing is expanding its campaign to dominate the South China Sea.

entry.title: How Latin America Became More Trump-Aligned Than Ever
entry.content: Anti-incumbent sentiment has swept into office right-wing leaders on board with Washington’s drug and migrant policies.

entry.title: Nonstop Trading, Loads of Leverage. How ‘Perp Futures’ Are Shaking Up Wall Street.
entry.content: The CFTC’s approval of so-called perps has triggered a selloff in U.S. exchanges’ shares—and at least one high-profile lawsuit.

entry.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open-source bugs
entry.content: OpenAI is attempting to tackle the security issues of the open source software community.

entry.title: We Parsed Trump’s Shifting Rhetoric on the Iran War
entry.content: The president initially said his goal was to dislodge the regime in Tehran, destroy its missile arsenal and curtail its nuclear program. Here’s how his views have changed.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Nothing is impossible to a willing heart.
Lucky Numbers: 33, 73, 60, 48, 79, 51

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use corn starch or baking soda on goopy or oily messes. It helps solidify it a bit, and then it's easier to 'scoop' it up and throw it away.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Mexico City sinks about 10 inches a year

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Unknown
entry.content: “You may be one person in this world, but to one person you may mean the world.” — Unknown

entry.title: Report: Dylan Larkin Trade Destination Revealed
entry.content: New reports suggest Dylan Larkin prefers a trade to the Dallas Stars. Here's what it could mean for the Detroit Red Wings.

entry.title: A black jaguar oracle in Rio de Janeiro sniffs Brazil to beat Scotland in Group C challenge
entry.content: A new animal oracle for the World Cup has arrived, and it's the kind that few would challenge even if its prediction is wrong. A black jaguar named Poty has made her pick at Rio de Janeiro's BioParque for the highly anticipated World Cup Group C clash between Brazil and Scotland in Miami on Wednesday. Residents and soccer fans watched closely on Monday as Poty took part in a lighthearted forecasting event, which placed her in front of two enrichment boxes representing the two rivals.

entry.title: Julian Alvarez makes transfer admission amid Arsenal and Barcelona links
entry.content: Julian Alvarez has told Atletico Madrid that he wants to leave the club in the summer transfer window to fulfill his “dream”. The Argentina international dropped the bombshell admission in an int...

entry.title: Trevor Story gives update on return to Red Sox, being ‘right on schedule’ after surgery
entry.content: On May 21, Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story had surgery to repair a sports hernia in Philadelphia. A month later, he’s on the mend — and in some ways, making strides more quickly than the team expected.

entry.title: Arsenal preparing to test Aston Villa with Morgan Rogers bid
entry.content: The Gunners are ready to make their move for Aston Villa forward Morgan Rogers as Mikel Arteta's summer rebuild begins to take shape.

entry.title: France vs. Iraq weather updates: Match delayed at halftime due to severe weather in Philadelphia
entry.content: Here's the latest on the rain delay in Philadelphia that struck during halftime of France vs. Iraq.

entry.title: Two-time NWSL MVP Temwa Chawinga signs extension with Kansas City Current
entry.content: Temwa Chawinga has signed a contract extension that will keep her with the Kansas City Current through the 2029 season.

entry.title: Remembering Clive Davis, who elevated hitmaking to an art form
entry.content: The veteran record executive didn't sing or play an instrument. But his instincts made him one of the surest spotters and nurturers of talent in pop history.

entry.title: Trump Administration Steps Up Law Enforcement Presence at Reflecting Pool
entry.content: The president alleged that vandals damaged the pool, but the administration has provided little evidence to support that claim.

entry.title: Top auto regulator opens special probe after a Tesla slams into a Texas home, killing a 76-year-old
entry.content: The top U.S. auto regulator opened an investigation Monday after a Tesla using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside.

entry.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
entry.content: Whether the Autopilot system was truly active, overridden, or malfunctioning likely won't be resolved until investigators finish combing through the vehicle's data logs.

entry.title: Shareholders sue Uber’s board over sexual assaults, other incidents
entry.content: The lawsuit, led by a Detroit pension fund, alleges Uber's board and management has cut too many compliance corners, resulting in thousands of lawsuits.

entry.title: Meta is 'pausing' employee tracking program after it let the whole company see sensitive data
entry.content: This won't make the already-controversial AI training endeavor any more popular.

entry.title: America's naval power transformation to be in spotlight at major Threat Status event
entry.content: U.S. naval power is undergoing its greatest transformation in a generation, and the nation's status as the top global military superpower may hinge on the outcome.

entry.title: The Forces That Broke the Two-Party System in the U.K.
entry.content: British politics has gone from predictable and patrician to utterly maverick. Now, the once unthinkable looks possible: an unpopular populist taking power.

entry.title: Federal Probe Finds Surfside Collapse Started Three Weeks Before Final Breakdown
entry.content: Government agency NIST concludes that an undetected structural failure in 2021 created a domino effect that ultimately claimed 98 lives.

entry.title: U.S. Allows Iran to Sell Oil in Dollars for First Time in Decades
entry.content: The decision temporarily dismantles sanctions imposed on Tehran’s oil industry, making it easier for the regime to repatriate profits from its crude exports.

entry.title: Domino’s Pizza Picks Insider as New CEO
entry.content: Plus, the U.S. will allow Iran to sell oil in dollars, and the ‘Gaokao’ test strikes fear in China’s high-schoolers.

entry.title: Olivia Rodrigo is for the girls: All-women lineup set for her Daisy Chain Fields fest in Irvine
entry.content: Olivia Rodrigo says the star-studded, all-women lineup for her Daisy Chain Fields music festival is "truly insane and full of my heroes and friends."

entry.title: ESPN's 'SportsCenter' stalwart Linda Cohn is retiring
entry.content: Cohn joined the Walt Disney-owned network in 1992. Her final appearance is Friday.

entry.title: Fox embraces a hybrid ad approach during World Cup hydration breaks after backlash
entry.content: Fox kicked off the World Cup by airing full-screen ads during FIFA's newly introduced hydration breaks, sparking some outrage among viewers.

entry.title: Trump orders government to build powerful quantum computer
entry.content: President Trump on Monday directed the federal government to build a powerful quantum computer for scientific research and ordered the intelligence agencies to protect government systems from cyber threats.

entry.title: SpaceX, Fresh Off Its Historic IPO, Readies Its Bond-Market Debut
entry.content: The rocket company’s shares fell 16% amid reports it plans to sell at least $20 billion in bonds. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite fell 1.3%, while the Dow rose 0.3%.

entry.title: A plan to sell artifacts from the Titanic faces US government opposition
entry.content: A newly unsealed court filing shows the U.S. government opposes a plan to auction more than 100 artifacts recovered from the wreckage of the Titanic in the North Atlantic

entry.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
entry.content: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

entry.title: Operating Company of On the Border Mexican Grill Files for Chapter 7 Liquidation
entry.content: OTB Hospitality closed all company-owned locations but franchised restaurants continue to operate independently and aren’t part of the bankruptcy filing.

entry.title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
entry.content: Microsoft inked a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron, locking in decades of carbon emissions from a new natural gas power plant.

entry.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
entry.content: What does an AI company do after one of those not-acqui-hire deals? Groq raised money, is leaning into its neocloud business, and is hiring new execs.

entry.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
entry.content: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that cuts water use inside the data center. But it does nothing to address AI's biggest water use — fossil fuel power plants.

entry.title: Tata Electronics, a major tech supplier to Apple and Tesla, confirms data breach
entry.content: The incident comes as Tata Electronics expands its role in global technology supply chains.

entry.title: How to start strength training in midlife, according to female trainers 40 and over
entry.content: Experts share a few tools, from dumbbells to resistance bands to cooling towels, to help women get strongerSign up for the Filter US newsletter, your weekly guide to buying fewer, better thingsUnfortunately, muscle mass peaks long before wisdom does. After age 40, we lose about 3% to 8% of it per decade, and after 50 the losses accelerate. Known as sarcopenia, this decline means our muscles don’t respond to exercise the way they once did. For many women, perimenopause makes the uphill climb even steeper, making it tougher to build strength and recover after workouts.That doesn’t mean it’s pointless to exercise later in life – in fact, it’s fully the opposite. You may not get abs, but your health improves across the board when you strength train: “Regular aerobic and resistance training cuts the risk of almost every noncommunicable disease – type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, Alzheimer’s,” Leigh Breen, an expert in skeletal muscle physiology and metabolism at Birmingham University, previously told the Guardian.Stephanie Gaudreau: a certified personal trainer who founded Strong With Steph, a program that focuses on training women over 40Anne Marie Chaker: a professional bodybuilder and author of Lift, a book on weightlifting for womenCaroline Idiens, age 54: the founder of the popular fitness program Caroline’s Circuits and author of Fit at 50Kate Whetsel, age 51: a certified personal trainer and health coach, specializing in women going through perimenopauseCAP Neoprene Dumbbells Continue reading...

entry.title: A new unpatchable flaw in Apple chips opens the door to an iPhone jailbreak
entry.content: European offensive cybersecurity company Paradigm Shift released details of a flaw and a technique to exploit it that opens the door for hackers to unlock and break into older iPhones.

entry.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
entry.content: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

entry.title: Anthropic says Claude may want to see your ID
entry.content: Claude's chatbot may ask to verify your age and identity "in certain circumstances," such as with a passport or driver's license, according to a privacy policy change.

entry.title: House committee leaders strike elusive bipartisan deal on kids' online safety legislation
entry.content: House Energy and Commerce Committee leaders on Monday announced a bipartisan deal on a sweeping kids' online safety package that has stalled in the chamber for years amid partisan disagreements.

entry.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
entry.content: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.

entry.title: Canadian healthcare staff decry ‘cruel hoax’ after scam email promises paid day off
entry.content: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and LabradorFor years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point.So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

entry.title: Supreme Court Reinstates Conviction in Murder of New York City Boy Etan Patz
entry.content: An appeals court had ruled that Pedro Hernandez deserved new trial in the 1979 disappearance of 6-year-old.

entry.title: Draper 'wins ugly like Murray' on injury return
entry.content: Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months.

entry.title: Why Paddy Pimblett can see Conor McGregor knocking out Max Holloway
entry.content: Paddy Pimblett thinks welterweight gives Conor McGregor a major advantage at UFC 329.

entry.title: Transfer latest on five midfielders either joining or leaving Manchester City this summer
entry.content: Manchester City are continuing to face a string of challenges and decisions in the midfield department this summer.Sporting director Hugo Viana is expected to sign at least one marquee name in the cen...

entry.title: Jay Shah says motherhood should not end cricket careers as ICC introduces new guidelines
entry.content: The ICC has introduced new post-pregnancy return-to-play guidelines to help female cricketers continue their careers after childbirth. The framework includes a six-step recovery model and support measures such as flexible training and childcare assistance. ICC chairman Jay Shah said the initiative reflects the governing body's commitment to ensuring women can thrive both on and off the field.

entry.title: Why do some World Cup shirts keep tearing during matches?
entry.content: Puma shirts have torn four times during the World Cup so far, so what exactly is going on?

entry.title: Here's when, and where, Saints hold joint practices with other teams
entry.content: Here's when, and where, the New Orleans Saints will hold joint practices with their preseason opponents after training camp this summer.

entry.title: Itauma handed everything on a plate - Hrgovic
entry.content: A tetchy Filip Hrgovic said British heavyweight Moses Itauma has been handed opportunities on a "plate" in a tense news conference in London.

entry.title: Follow Eastbourne and Wimbledon qualifying on the BBC
entry.content: Jack Draper makes his return at Eastbourne and Wimbledon qualifying gets under way - follow it across the BBC.

entry.title: A24 takes on Google money build AI tools
entry.content: A24 is taking $75 million in Google money to help build AI tools for movie production.

entry.title: Can You Pass the Test That Strikes Fear Into China’s High-Schoolers?
entry.content: Try a selection of questions from the notoriously difficult “Gaokao” university entrance exam.

entry.title: Tesla in autopilot crashed into Texas home, killing one
entry.content: One woman is dead after a Tesla with "an automated driving assistance system" engaged crashed into a home in Katy, Texas.

entry.title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
entry.content: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

entry.title: 'Fungalpunk' RPG Signet City isn't afraid to be weird and political
entry.content: Gareth Damian Martin drew on diverse inspirations for Signet City, from horror manga to a musical from Sting.

entry.title: Instagram for TV is now available on Samsung TVs in the US
entry.content: Instagram's TV app is expanding to Samsung TVs in the US, half a year after it launched for Amazon Fire TV devices.

entry.title: Manchester United acquire land to build new 100,000 seater stadium
entry.content: Manchester United say they have secured the majority of the land required to build a new 100,000-seater stadium.The new stadium is planned to be the biggest sporting arena in the UK, with the club acq...

entry.title: Named George Washington? This World Cup promo could get you a Jeep
entry.content: Jeep is going to award 100 vehicles to eligible adults named George Washington, if Team USA wins the World Cup. But there are caveats.

entry.title: Former Wimbledon champion Vondrousova suspended 4 years for refusing doping test
entry.content: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova was suspended for four years on Monday for refusing an anti-doping test — even though the Czech player cited “mental stress” and fear when the testing agent “rang my door late at night without properly identifying themselves.” The International Tennis Integrity Agency made the announcement, saying that Vondrousova refused a test in December, and that the decision was reached by an independent tribunal. Vondrousova, who won Wimbledon in 2023, reached a career-high ranking of No. 6 that year.

entry.title: Pirates Slide In Latest MLB Power Rankings, Playoff Chances Dicey
entry.content: The Pittsburgh Pirates (39-39) are in fourth place in the National League Central, 8.5 games behind the first-place Milwaukee Brewers.

entry.title: Brandon Aiyuk just wants the 49ers to mercifully fire him already
entry.content: Another week, another dicey social media post from the soon-to-be ex-Niner.

entry.title: 2026 World Cup today: Watch superstars Messi, Mbappé, Haaland play in matches on Boston 25
entry.content: Don’t miss a packed day of World Cup action, all leading into Boston Stadium's next big match on Tuesday.

entry.title: OpenAI signs deal to show Getty's images in ChatGPT results
entry.content: Getty Images will allow OpenAI to use its content library in AI search and ChatGPT.

entry.title: Chinese-style EV battery swap stations are coming to Europe
entry.content: Octopus and CATL are pledging to roll out a network of battery swap stations for heavy trucks across Europe.

entry.title: New York's congressional candidates make final case in last day before primary
entry.content: New York’s congressional candidates will deliver closing arguments ahead of primary elections Tuesday, as an ascendent progressive left takes on establishment Democrats
